rationale: The only change was employer names (re:cinq/Optilyz/GISCollective vs. generic Acme/Pinepoint/Northwind), which carry no job-relevant signal here. Both evaluations land on the identical score of 6/10 and the same core narrative: some-but-not-recent C++ experience, strong general software background, and no capital-markets/fixed-income exposure. The differences — C++ surfacing as an explicit strength, SQL framed as a concern about SQL Server/Oracle rather than a strength, and the capital-markets point migrating into the justification — are reshuffling and rewording of the same substance, well within ordinary run-to-run variation. None of the shifts key off the swapped company names or any age/nationality/location signal.
